papers.title: Collective effects in O-O and Ne-Ne collisions at $\sqrt{{s}_{NN}}=5.36$ TeV from a hybrid approach

papers.summary: Author(s): Lucas Constantin, Niklas Götz, Carl B. Rosenkvist, and Hannah ElfnerMany features of heavy-ion collisions are well described by hybrid approaches, where the droplet of strongly coupled quark gluon plasma (QGP) is modeled by hydrodynamics and the subsequent dilute stage is performed with a hadronic transport model. papers.title: Investigation of the $\overline{K}\text{−}^{6}\mathrm{Li}$ interaction and the search for the $\mathrm{Λ}$(1405) resonance
papers.authors: Ahmad Naderi Beni and Sajjad Marri
papers.summary: Author(s): Ahmad Naderi Beni and Sajjad MarriWe investigate the interaction of an antikaon (${K}^{−}$) with the $^{6}\mathrm{Li}$ nucleus, described as an $α+d$ cluster system. The study aims to explore the formation of the $\mathrm{Λ}(1405)$ resonance through the ${K}^{−}d$ subsystem in the presence of a spectator $α$ particle. In the absence…[Phys. Rev. C 113, 055201] Published Fri May 01, 2026

papers.title: ${K}^{*}(892)$ resonance suppression in $\mathrm{Ar}+\mathrm{Sc}$ collisions at energies available at the CERN Super Proton Synchrotron
papers.authors: Amine Chabane, Tom Reichert, Jan Steinheimer, and Marcus Bleicher
papers.summary: Author(s): Amine Chabane, Tom Reichert, Jan Steinheimer, and Marcus BleicherWe investigate the production and suppression of short-lived ${K}^{*}(892)$ resonances in $\mathrm{p}+\mathrm{p}$ and $\mathrm{Ar}+\mathrm{Sc}$ collisions at CERN-SPS energies ($\sqrt{{s}_{\mathrm{NN}}}=8.8$, 11.9, and 16.8 GeV) using the Ultrarelativistic Quantum Molecular dynamics (UrQMD) model.
